message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Edge Steakhouse (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 315 reviews; Tripadvisor 187 reviews (#8 of 80 Rancho Mirage); OpenTable ~1500+ reviews [UNVERIFIED exact count]
- Sentiment: mostly positive, with mixed service notes
- Longevity: Opened January 2015 at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (per GlobeNewswire press release). Current executive chef Nyitre Rodgers. Stable resort ownership under Marriott/Ritz-Carlton brand.
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor #8 of 80 Rancho Mirage restaurants (4.4/5)","Featured in Palm Springs Life steakhouse roundup","Featured in Locale Magazine date-night and steakhouse roundups"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', 'fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'restaurants with a view Rancho Mirage', and 'Coachella Valley steakhouse'. Often appears second to The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente in award-led queries (because Agua Caliente owns the Wine Spectator/Forbes Four-Star/AAA Four Diamond credentials). Wins view/ambiance queries; loses pure-credential queries.
- Site structured data: The Ritz-Carlton dining page (ritzcarlton.com/.../the-edge-steakhouse) presents clean hours, cuisine, dress code, phone, and address in human-readable form but no JSON-LD/schema.org Restaurant markup detected in fetched content. Also listed on Marriott Bonvoy dining directory and OpenTable (which provides its own structured data layer). Discovery surfaces are strong for agents that crawl Marriott/Ritz/OpenTable/Yelp; the venue does not appear to operate an independent domain (theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas brand, increasing entity-disambiguation risk for agents).
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here are the search results for The Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage:

## Overview
The Edge at the Ritz Carlton in Rancho Mirage is a beautiful restaurant with a great view. It's the only cliffside restaurant overlooking the valley.

## Ratings & Reviews
On Tripadvisor, The Edge Steakhouse is rated 4.4 of 5 and ranked #9 of 67 restaurants in Rancho Mirage. OpenTable shows 1,502 reviews with one being described as "One of the best dining experiences of the year. A destination of its own".

## Dining Experience
The Edge offers an exceptional dining experience with breathtaking views and phenomenal service. The menu includes a selection of carefully chosen seafood, and the vegan options are of the highest quality. 

## Chef & Management
Chef Nyitre Rodgers' impressive career includes collaborations with Marcus Samuelsson, José Andrés, and Chef Jean-Georges.

## Notable Feedback
The petit filet is described as one of the best steaks one reviewer has had in a long time. However, some reviews mention that food was excellent, but slow to come out between courses, though wait staff was very attentive and accommodating.

Based on the 2026 search results, here are the best steakhouses in Rancho Mirage:

## Top Steakhouses in Rancho Mirage (2026)

**The Edge Steakhouse**
The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is a beacon of exceptional American cuisine, renowned for its exquisite steaks and seafood, located within The Ritz-Carlton. Unlike any other restaurant in Greater Palm Springs, this one sits 650 feet above the valley, offering sweeping views of the adjacent mountain range. The steaks, particularly the Wagyu, are frequently praised as "the best we've ever had".

**The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente**
The Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award, and winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007. At Agua Caliente Casinos, The Steakhouse offers fine dining with top ribeye, seafood, delicious sides, specialty cocktails, and excellent service.

**Other Notable Options**
Other steakhouses near Rancho Mirage include Foxy's Kitchen + Bar, Kobe Japanese Steak House, Eddie V's Prime Seafood, Mastro's Steakhouse, and LG's.

Both The Edge Steakhouse and The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente stand out as premier choices for upscale steakhouse dining in Rancho Mirage.

I found relevant information about The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage from a GlobeNewsWire press release. Here are the key details:

The Edge Steakhouse, located inside The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, opened to the public in January 2015 near Palm Springs, California. Set cliff-side, 650 feet above the desert floor, The Edge is a dramatic setting.

The culinary team at The Edge crafts remarkable prime steaks, both premium hand-cut and dry-aged bone-in meats and seafood paired with an international wine list. The restaurant features a dry-aging room, where select steaks are aged for 21-65 days, a process that tenderizes beef and concentrates the natural flavors.

The steakhouse seats 82 in the glassed-in dining room with an additional 12 seats at the bar.

I've found excellent information about fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage perfect for date nights with views. Here are some top recommendations:

## The Edge Steakhouse

The Edge, with sweeping views of Rancho Mirage, crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley. This one sits 650 feet above the valley, offering sweeping views of the adjacent mountain range.

The Edge Steakhouse is a top choice for a romantic date night, with guests repeatedly praising the panoramic sunset views, intimate dining room and attentive, celebratory service that make it ideal for anniversaries and special evenings. The Edge offers patio/outdoor seating with sweeping views of the valley, with guests frequently describing sitting on the patio to watch the sunset, enjoying heaters on cool nights and praising the dramatic vista from outdoor tables.

## Wally's Desert Turtle

Founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is a bona fide piece of Rancho Mirage history, revered as the origin of fine dining in the desert, offering up a five-star experience. Wally's Desert Turtle is a Rancho Mirage institution that delivers timeless elegance with every white-linen detail, pairing refined California cuisine with a glamorous setting—mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents.

## Shame on the Moon

Since its doors opened in 1984, Shame on the Moon has been a fine-dining date night hot spot, with an intimate, ornate and elegant dual dining rooms.

## Other Notable Options

Rancho Mirage has a wide array of world-class restaurants boasting sweeping panoramic mountain views and some of the best carefully curated menus in the area. Additional options include Willie's Modern Fare, which blends classic sophistication with a fresh, contemporary twist, offering a polished dining experience that feels equal parts stylish and inviting.

# Structured Data Analysis for The Edge Steakhouse

Based on my examination of the provided HTML content, **no JSON-LD or schema.org Restaurant markup is present** for The Edge Steakhouse or any other restaurant at this property.

## Available Information (Unstructured)

The page does contain relevant details about The Edge Steakhouse presented in standard HTML format:

- **Hours**: "Thu-Sun: 5:00 PM-10:00 PM" (closed Monday-Wednesday)
- **Phone**: +1 760-202-6180
- **Address**: Located at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, 68900 Frank Sinatra Drive, Rancho Mirage, California 92270
- **Description**: "Set on a cliff above the Coachella Valley, this modern Palm Springs steakhouse" offering "global steaks, seafood, craft cocktails"

The restaurant information exists only as readable HTML text rather than machine-readable structured data markup.

# The Edge Steakhouse - Tripadvisor Rankings

Based on the webpage content, here are The Edge Steakhouse's current Tripadvisor metrics:

**Ranking:** "#8 of 80 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage"

**Star Rating:** 4.4 out of 5 bubbles

**Review Count:** 187 reviews

The restaurant is classified as a steakhouse and seafood establishment in the highest price category ($$$$), and holds a Travelers' Choice award designation.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Edge Steakhouse at Westgate Las Vegas:

## Overview

Edge Steakhouse has been voted as the #1 steakhouse in Las Vegas and offers an extensive menu blending traditional American steakhouse favorites with specialty appetizers, entrées and side dishes.

## Location and Contact

Edge Steakhouse is located at Westgate Las Vegas Resort and Casino at 3000 Paradise Rd, Las Vegas, Nevada, 89109. You can reach them at 702.732.5277 or westgateresorts.com.

## Menu & Dining Experience

The menu features top quality wet and dry-aged Prime cuts, Domestic Wagyu Beef, fresh seafood, and several other mouth-watering specialties. Diners can find favorites, sample prix fixe options, or savor the 5-course chef tasting dinner, with a double Wine Spectator Award winning wine list.

## Atmosphere

The restaurant features a lush modern environment with dark woods and red plush seating, designed to make you feel relaxed and ready to enjoy world-class cocktails and cuisine.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about The Edge Steakhouse:

The Edge Steakhouse is located in Rancho Mirage and can be found at theedgesteakhouse.com. 

**Location Details:**
The restaurant is located at 68900 Frank Sinatra Drive, Rancho Mirage, California, USA 92270, and operates Thursday-Sunday from 5:30 PM-9:30 PM.

**Restaurant Features:**
The Edge Steakhouse is located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage. The steakhouse offers sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list.

**Amenities:**
The restaurant features floor-to-ceiling glass-enclosed wine cellars with a curated selection balancing Old and New World labels, from Bordeaux and Barolo to Napa Valley and Willamette Valley wines.

**Dining Hours:**
The restaurant is now open Monday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10 p.m. (Note: There appears to be a discrepancy in the hours listed on different pages of their website).

content.text: Confirmed: theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Rancho Mirage Ritz-Carlton location (68900 Frank Sinatra Dr) — NOT Westgate Las Vegas. The claimed disambiguation risk is wrong; the venue DOES operate an independent domain.

confirms: Yelp 315 reviews (confirmed via Yelp result snippet showing '496 Photos & 315 Reviews')
Tripadvisor 187 reviews and 4.4/5 rating (confirmed via Tripadvisor fetch)
Opened January 2015 at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (confirmed via GlobeNewswire press release dated 2015/01/16)
Executive chef Nyitre Rodgers (confirmed via multiple sources including 5reasonstovisit.com and search snippets)

Surfaces strongly for 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', 'fine dining Rancho Mirage', and date-night/view queries (confirmed — appears prominently in OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Locale Magazine, and Visit Greater Palm Springs results)
The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente competes on credentials (Forbes Four-Star, Wine Spectator since 2007) — confirms the credentialed-competitor framing
Featured in Locale Magazine date-night and steakhouse roundups (confirmed via localemagazine.com/rancho-mirage-restaurants and localemagazine.com/best-steakhouse-in-rancho-mirage)
Marriott Bonvoy dining directory and OpenTable listings present (confirmed via direct search results)
Ritz-Carlton dining page has no JSON-LD/schema.org Restaurant markup (confirmed via WebFetch of ritzcarlton.com dining page — content is human-readable only)
contradicts: Claim: 'the venue does not appear to operate an independent domain (theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas brand)' — ACTUALLY FALSE. theedgesteakhouse.com is the Rancho Mirage Ritz-Carlton's own site (confirms address 68900 Frank Sinatra Dr, Rancho Mirage; pages titled 'Steakhouses in Palm Springs California | The Edge Steakhouse', 'The Edge Steakhouse in Palm Springs'). The Westgate Las Vegas property uses westgateresorts.com/.../edge-steakhouse/, NOT theedgesteakhouse.com. The venue DOES operate an independent domain, materially improving its agent-discoverability and reducing the claimed disambiguation risk.

Claim: 'Tripadvisor #8 of 80 Rancho Mirage' — Tripadvisor currently shows '#8 of 80' per direct fetch, BUT one search snippet showed '#9 of 67'. Ranking is mostly confirmed (#8 of 80 matches direct fetch) so this is borderline; flagging the volatility but the headline claim holds.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable ~1500+ reviews — one search snippet referenced '1,502 reviews' which directionally supports the claim, but OpenTable fetch timed out so could not independently verify the live count
Palm Springs Life steakhouse roundup feature — directory.palmspringslife.com listing confirmed but specific 'steakhouse roundup' feature article not independently located
Sentiment 'mostly positive with mixed service notes' — directionally supported by review snippets mentioning slow pacing but attentive staff, but not systematically verified across review corpus
Visibility gap score of 5 and convertibility tier 'anchor-reference' — these are internal scoring constructs, not externally verifiable
Whether Marriott/OpenTable provide adequate structured-data layer for agents — partially observable (OpenTable likely has its own schema) but not directly inspected in this audit
input.notes: The single most consequential error in the assessment is the domain-ownership claim: theedgesteakhouse.com is the Rancho Mirage venue's own website (Palm Springs/Rancho Mirage branding, Frank Sinatra Dr address), NOT Westgate Las Vegas (which uses westgateresorts.com). This flips a key finding — the venue DOES have an independent domain, reducing disambiguation risk for agents and improving its standalone discoverability. The visibility-gap score of 5 may be too pessimistic given this correction. Other quantitative claims (Yelp 315, TA 187, #8 of 80, 4.4/5, 2015 open, Chef Rodgers) all verified cleanly. Ritz-Carlton dining page confirmed to lack JSON-LD as claimed.
